2020.policy_notes: Pre-March 2021 policy. Formula inferred -- PART-I dominates at 40 marks. Total: 95 marks.
policy_tidbits: PART-I is the dominant component at 40 marks -- FCPS/MD/MS performance is critical.
Matric + Intermediate contribute 12 marks -- early academic record counts.
Total out of 95 -- a closing merit of 85.68 corresponds to ~90.2% of the maximum.
active_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2021.policy_notes: Original March 2021 PPM. PART-I (40 marks) + Matric/Inter/MBBS aggregate + house job + parent institute + experience + university positions. Total: 95 marks.
policy_tidbits: PART-I exam result is the largest single component at 40 marks -- FCPS-I pass awarded full 40 marks.
Matric (5) + Intermediate (7) = 12 marks -- early academic record matters.
MBBS aggregate (20 marks) rewards consistent professional exam performance.
University positions: 0.6 pts/position (MBBS), 0.75 pts/position (BDS) at university level only.
Total out of 95, not 100. Observed max: 80.68 (Jan 2021), 80.68 (Jul 2021).
active_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2022.policy_notes: Same March 2021 PPM formula. Total: 95 marks. Observed max: 77.93 (Jan 2022), 80.61 (Jul 2022).
policy_tidbits: Formula identical to 2021 -- consistent year-on-year for inductions 9--14.
PART-I (40 marks) + Matric + Inter + MBBS aggregate dominates the formula.
Jan 2022 observed max: 77.93. Jul 2022 observed max: 80.61.
active_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2023.policy_notes: Same formula as 2021--2022. Highest observed max in PART-I era: 87.94 (Jan 2023). Total: 95 marks.
policy_tidbits: Highest observed max merit in PART-I era: 87.94 in Jan 2023 -- most competitive PART-I cohort.
Formula unchanged from previous two years -- strong cross-year comparability.
Jul 2023 observed max: 84.98 -- slight decrease from Jan 2023.
active_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2024.policy_notes: May 2024 PPM superseded all prior policies but maintained the same formula at 95 marks. Jan 2024 amendment (attempts_in_mbbs) was short-lived.
policy_tidbits: New PPM issued May 2024 officially repealed March 2021 policy, but scoring formula is unchanged.
Jan 2024 amendment briefly added 'Attempts in MBBS' (5 marks, total 100) -- then removed in May 2024 PPM.
To model Jan 2024 (Ind 15): enable 'attempts_in_mbbs' and set total_marks to 100.
Observed max 2024: 85.92 out of 95 -- consistent with prior PART-I era years.
active_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2025.policy_notes: MAJOR change: PART-I (40 marks) REMOVED. Hard Area Service (5 marks) added. Total drops from 95 -> 60 marks. Raw scores NOT comparable to prior years -- use Centile view.
policy_tidbits: Biggest formula change in PRP history: PART-I exam removed entirely (was 40/95 marks = 42% of total).
New component: Hard Area Service -- 1.25 pts per 3 months at notified BHUs/RHCs, max 5 pts (1 year).
Total maximum is now 60 marks -- raw scores directly incomparable to 2021--2024 data.
Use Centile view (% of max) for cross-year comparison: observed max ~46.6 = 77.7% of 60.
Hard Area and Experience both to be reduced/discontinued from Jan 2026 (Ind 20).
removed_components: FCPS Part-I / MD / MS / MDS Part-I

2026.policy_notes: Slightly changed from Induction 20 (Dec/Jan 2026) . Matric/Inter/PART-I/Parent Institute all removed. Experience Marks discontinued. Total: 30 marks.
policy_tidbits: Complete overhaul: total drops from 60 to 30 marks. Formula components restructured.
MDCAT introduced (2 marks) -- benefits recent graduates who scored well on MDCAT.
FCPS/JCAT: now 5 marks pass/fail (was 40 marks score-based in 2020--2024) -- massive reduction.
Matric + Intermediate removed -- 12 marks that no longer matter for merit.
Parent institute bonus removed -- where you graduated no longer gives marks.
Hard Area service  and Experience marks discontinued -- rural service no longer rewarded.
